findings.message: PII (owner_email) is exposed in the ItemRead response model, directly contradicting the explicit protection requirement stated in the model comment 'do not include in logs or external responses'
findings.suggestion: Remove owner_email from ItemRead response model or create a separate internal model for storage. If client display is needed, implement explicit field filtering via Pydantic Field(exclude=True) or create a separate response schema without PII.

findings.message: owner_email is collected in ItemCreate request but never used by the post_item endpoint, representing unnecessary PII collection
findings.suggestion: Either use the owner_email field in create_item() or remove it from ItemCreate to prevent unnecessary PII collection.

findings.message: post_item endpoint accepts owner_email PII but ignores it, passing only name to create_item(). This suggests incomplete implementation of PII handling.
findings.suggestion: If owner_email should be stored, pass it to create_item(). If it should not be exposed, remove it from ItemRead response model or implement filtering.
